title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 54.
content:
There was a Young Lady of Lucca,
    Whose lovers completely forsook her;
    She ran up a tree,
    And said, "Fiddle-de-dee!"
    Which embarrassed the people of Lucca.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Edward Lear delivers a powerful performance in "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 54."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
